LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO APRIL-30-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO GOVERN PRODUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATIONS. THE COURT GRANTS IN PART AND DENIES IN PART FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; COURT REPORTER: RAQUEL SHARP, CSR # 10619 (TEL: 925-226-8222 / EMAIL: RAQUEL@TRANSCRIPTLEGAL.COM) =(301/CVA)

DA FONSECA; COURT REPORTER: RAQUEL SHARP, CSR# 10619 (TEL: 925-954-9888 / EMAIL: RAQUEL@TRANSCRIPTLEGAL.COM). =(301/CVA) ***** AFTER THE HEARING THE COURT SIGNS AN ORDER GRANTING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INSPECTION DEMANDS AND DENYING SANCTIONS (SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING)*****

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO JUNE-03-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF SAMUEL CHAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES FROM DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF SAMUEL CHAN MOVES FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO HIS SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES TO FORD MOTOR COMPANY. THE COURT FINDS FORD'S OBJECTIONS TO THESE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES WELL TAKEN. CHAN DOES NOT EXPLAIN HOW FORD'S ESTIMATES OF REPAIR RATES FOR FORD EXPLORERS, OR RECURRING PROBLEMS WITH FORD EXPLORERS, BEAR ON THE CLAIMS OR DEFENSES AT ISSUE IN THIS CASE. THE MOTION IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; COURT REPORTER: RAQUEL SHARP, CSR# 10619 (TEL: 925-954-9888 / EMAIL: RAQUEL@TRANSCRIPTLEGAL.COM). =(301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
Motion For Attorneys? Fees, Costs And Expenses
Off Calendar Jun-17-2026 Text Ruling
TENTATIVE RULING:
Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2026, Line 4, PLAINTIFF SAMUEL CHAN's Motion For Attorneys' Fees, Costs And Expenses.

(Complete tentative ruling e-mailed to the parties).

Plaintiff Samuel Chan moves for an award of attorneys fees following his settlement of Song-Beverly Act litigation against Ford Motor Company. The court awards fees of $23,488 plus costs in the amount of $5764.60 for the reasons stated herein.

For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES, COSTS AND EXPENSES

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Samuel Chan moves for an award of attorneys fees following his settlement of Song-Beverly Act litigation against Ford Motor Company. The court awards fees of $23,488 plus costs in the amount of $5764.60 for the reasons stated herein.

"Determining a reasonable attorney fee award in Song-Beverly Act cases ordinarily begins with the 'lodestar,' which is the number of hours reasonably expended multiplied by the reasonable hourly rate. The lodestar may then be adjusted based on factors specific to the case, in order to fix the fee at the fair market value of the legal services provided. The lodestar method anchors the trial court's analysis to an objective determination of the value of the attorney's services, and thus ensures the amount awarded is not arbitrary." (Tidrick v. FCA US LLC (2025) 112 Cal.App.5th 1147, 1157-1158 [citation simplified].) "The Song-Beverly Act's attorney fee provision requires the trial court to make an initial determination of the actual time expended; and then to ascertain whether under all the circumstances of the case the amount of actual time expended, and the monetary charge being made for the time expended are reasonable. These circumstances may include, but are not limited to, factors such as the complexity of the case and procedural demands, the skill exhibited, and the results achieved. If the time expended or the monetary charge being made for the time expended are not reasonable under all the circumstances, then the court must take this into account and award attorney fees in a lesser amount. A prevailing buyer has the burden of showing that the fees incurred were allowable, were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation, and were reasonable in amount. (Id., at p. 1158 [citation simplified].)

The court concludes that Chan's attorneys' billing records are reasonable in their hourly rates, based on the court's experience with similar litigation, and are reasonable in the amount of hours expended. Ford criticizes the number of billers, but the court credits Chan's counsel's declaration that the firm uses an assembly-line method for Song-Beverly litigation, with different counsel specializing in different steps in the process. The court does not award a multiplier, concluding that it is not justified here. With respect to costs, they appear proper on their face and Ford does not show that any item of costs was improper. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories From Defendant Ford Motor Company, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-03-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. SUNDEEP SAMRA, Esq. of Knight Law Group (tel: 310-552-2250) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. HANNAH BIEMANN, Esq. of Mortenson Taggart Adams LLP (tel: 949-774-2224) for the Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF SAMUEL CHAN's Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories From Defendant Ford Motor Company, And Request For Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Samuel Chan moves for an order compelling further responses to his special interrogatories to Ford Motor Company. The court finds Ford's objections to these special interrogatories well taken. Chan does not explain how Ford's estimates of repair rates for Ford Explorers, or recurring problems with Ford Explorers, bear on the claims or defenses at issue in this case. The motion is denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR# 10619 (tel: 925-954-9888 / email: raquel@transcriptlegal.com). =(301/CVA)

Motion For Protective Order To Govern Production Of
Confidential Materials
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DEBRA REED, Esq. of Knight Law Group (tel: 310-552-2250) for Plaintiff.
Atty. EMMA MAGGIO, Esq. of Mortenson Taggart Adams LLP (tel: 949-774-2224) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's Motion For Protective Order To Govern Production Of Confidential Materials.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications.

The court grants in part and denies in part Ford Motor Company's motion for protective order.

The parties are in agreement, and Ford's evidence establishes, that a protective order is appropriate in this case. The court adopts the version of the Los Angeles Superior Court model protective order attached as Exhibits F and G to the Maggio Declaration with the following modifications:

In paragraph 7.b., the court adopts the "same firm" limitation in the first sentence. This allows any counsel of record in this proceeding (including attorneys from other firms who take on representation of plaintiff Samuel Chan or defendant Ford and enter general appearances) to access the confidential materials. The court rejects the requirement that lawyers and staff within a firm representing Chan or Ford must execute a confidentiality agreement in the last sentence of paragraph 7.b.

In paragraph 7.d., the court adopts the changes Ford proposes. This is a common protective measure in commercial litigation for confidential material.

In paragraph 7.f., the court rejects the changes Ford proposes.

In paragraph 7.g., the court adopts the changes Ford proposes.

The court adopts Ford's new proposed 8.a. and 8.b. paragraphs.

The court adopts the changes Ford proposes to paragraph 21, except that plaintiff may return or certify the destruction within 60 days. This is another common protective measure in commercial litigation for confidential material.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR # 10619 (tel: 925-226-8222 / email: raquel@transcriptlegal.com) =(301/CVA)
